name: Potatoes. - Fourteen Ways of Dressing Them.
recipeInstructions:
1. Boiled Potatoes
- Wash potatoes thoroughly but do not peel or cut them unless they are very large.
- Fill a saucepan about halfway with water and add enough to cover the potatoes by an inch once placed in the pan.
- Place on moderate heat until boiling, then reduce to simmer gently.
- Cook just covered; avoid drowning.
- Drain carefully (do not let them sit after done), uncover, and allow excess moisture to evaporate.
2. Cold Potatoes Fried
- Melt a small amount of clean dripping in a frying pan.
- Slice potatoes thinly with pepper and salt.
- Cook over medium heat until hot, then drain on a folded napkin.
3. Boiled and Broiled Potatoes
- Prepare as boiled.
- Place on gridiron over very clear fire; brown all sides.
- Serve with melted butter in cup.
4. Fried Whole Potatoes
- When nearly cooked (as per step 1), add a little butter or beef dripping to the pan.
- Fry until brown and crisp, draining off fat.
5. Floured/Fried Potatoes
- Flour then dip in egg yolk; roll in breadcrumbs before frying as above.
6. Mashed Potatoes
- Drain boiled potatoes well, removing any debris.
- While hot, mash through a sieve into stewpan.
- Add about 1/2 ounce butter and 1 tablespoon milk per pound of potatoes; mix until moist but not soggy.
7. Mashed Potatoes with Onions
- Blend boiled onions after straining.
- Mix with mashed potatoes adding butter, pepper, and salt to taste.
8. Excaloped Potatoes
- Mash as in step 6.
- Butter scallop shells or pans; fill evenly.
- Brown on top then carefully lift out.
9. Colcannon
- Boil separate batches of potatoes and greens (spinach).
- Drain, mash, mix with chopped greens seasoned lightly.
- Shape into mound in greased oven dish; let sit 10 minutes before baking.
10. Roasted Potatoes
- Dry washed potatoes (all same size).
- Place in a Dutch oven or toaster away from direct heat.
- Cook for two hours until fully warmed through and lightly browned on the outside.
11. Under Meat Potatoes
- Partially boil large potatoes, drain excess water.
- Position under roasting meat; baste with drippings as they brow.
- Serve alongside or in serving dish.
12. Potato Balls
- Combine mashed potatoes and egg yolk; roll into balls.
- Optionally coat in flour then breadcrumbs before frying or browned in oven.
13. Potato Snow
- Use whitest, spot-free potatoes.
- Place in cold water until they crack.
- Drain, pass through sieve onto serving dish without further handling.
14. Potato Pie
- Slice potatoes paper-thin into pie dish.
- Layer with chopped onion (¼ oz for each lb of potato), pepper, salt, and small bits of butter.
- Cover with puff pastry; bake in moderate oven until set, about 1.5 hours.
